title: genista pulchella subsp. aquilana
intro: Genista pulchella subsp. aquilana, also known as the Italian broom, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, as it contains alkaloids and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Genista pulchella subsp. aquilana include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and support immune system function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
